home.helloAria: Hello, world — a small tradition for luck
home.title: Ship Next.js apps faster, with AI guardrails baked in.
home.subtitle: A production-grade Next.js 16 template. Supabase auth + RLS, full i18n, dark mode, accessibility, performance budgets, and an AGENTS.md hub that makes AI coding agents pull their weight.

features.sectionSubtitle: Every default in this template exists to save you a class of mistake. Open AGENTS.md or CONCEPTS.md for the full story.

auth.title: Auth & RLS
auth.description: Supabase SSR with a 4-client split. Row-Level Security is the primary authorization boundary.

aiNative.title: AI-Native
aiNative.description: AGENTS.md, CONCEPTS.md, MCP config, Cursor rules, Claude slash commands, prompt-context script — all built in.

performance.title: Performance
performance.description: Server Components first, lazy loading, Core Web Vitals targets, and bundle-budget diagnostics in the strict QA loop.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: English, Portuguese, and Spanish ready. Add a locale by editing one file.

a11y.title: WCAG 2.2 AA
a11y.description: Mobile-first, keyboard navigation, focus rings, color-not-only state — defaults that pass an audit.

qaLoop.title: QA in loop
qaLoop.description: npm run qa cycles format, text hygiene, lint, typecheck, test, and build until every gate is green.

stack.sectionSubtitle: Modern, opinionated, and boring on purpose.

about.metaDescription: What the Next.js bootstrap template gives you out of the box: the stack, the conventions, the agent surface, and the QA loop.
about.title: A Next.js 16 bootstrap built for humans and AI agents.
about.subtitle: This template wires the boring, production-grade defaults so you can focus on product. Everything below is opinionated on purpose; every default exists to prevent a class of mistake.

auth.title: Auth & RLS
auth.description: Supabase SSR, 4-client split, Row-Level Security as the primary authorization boundary.

aiNative.title: AI-Native
aiNative.description: AGENTS.md hub, .agents/ rules, MCP config, Cursor + Claude + Cline + Codex + Aider adapters.

performance.title: Performance
performance.description: Server Components first, lazy loading, per-route First Load JS budget enforced by CI.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: next-intl with en / pt / es bundles. Browser locale detected on first visit; user choice persisted.

a11y.title: WCAG 2.2 AA
a11y.description: Keyboard navigation, focus rings, semantic HTML, axe-core sweeps in the visual QA loop.

qaLoop.title: QA in loop
qaLoop.description: npm run qa cycles format → lint → typecheck → test → build until every gate is green.

theming.title: Theming
theming.description: Tailwind v4 @theme tokens, light / dark via next-themes, no inline hex — design tokens only.

docs.title: Docs in repo
docs.description: Every directory has a README index; canonical rules live in .agents/, adapters point back to AGENTS.md.

purpose.title: What this template is
purpose.body: A production-grade starter for full-stack Next.js apps. It bundles the decisions you'd otherwise spend a sprint making: auth strategy, i18n, theming, accessibility, performance budgets, observability, transactional email, and a documented playbook for AI coding agents. Generate an app from it, delete what you don't need, ship the rest.

stack.title: The stack
stack.body: Next.js 16 (App Router, React 19, TypeScript strict), Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui (radix-nova). Supabase via @supabase/ssr with a 4-client split (browser, server, server-admin, proxy). next-intl for i18n with en/pt/es. PostHog for product analytics with PII scrubbing baked in. Resend for transactional email. Zod 4 for schemas. Vitest + Playwright for tests. Pino for structured logging.

conventions.title: Conventions you do not have to invent
conventions.body: Server Components by default. No `select('*')`. Service role key is server-only (enforced by an import 'server-only' guard). All process.env access goes through src/env.ts (zod-validated at boot). Never hardcode user-facing strings; always use useTranslations / getTranslations. Path alias @/* only — no relative climb. Conventional Commits, husky pre-push gate that requires the auto-generated CHANGELOG entry.

agentSurface.title: The agent surface
agentSurface.body: AGENTS.md at the repo root is the routing hub. Canonical rules live in .agents/rules/, lookup material in .agents/references/, repeatable procedures in .agents/workflows/, installed skills in .agents/skills/. Per-tool files (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, .cursor/rules/*, .clinerules/*, .aider.conf.yml, .codex/setup.sh) are thin adapters that point back to AGENTS.md. Two duplicated files (.mcp.json and .cursor/mcp.json) are kept in sync by a CI gate.

qa.title: QA in the loop
qa.body: npm run qa runs every gate locally in cheapest-first order: prettier, text hygiene, mcp-sync, ESLint, tsc --noEmit, vitest, next build. The same script runs in GitHub Actions — local green means remote green. npm run qa:strict adds Playwright E2E, bundle-budget per route, and a visual QA sweep (route × locale × theme × viewport screenshots, axe-core WCAG 2.2 AA checks).

structure.title: Repo structure
structure.body: Every agent-facing directory carries a README index that lists its files and points back to AGENTS.md. Canonical content lives in .agents/; everything else is a thin adapter. Obligatory duplication (when tools require different filenames) is guarded by a sync gate in the QA loop. See .agents/references/repo-structure.md for the meta-rules and anti-patterns.
